name: Turbot with Fennel, Tomatoes, Olives, And
recipeIngredient: For the Preserved Lemon Zests: 8 lemons
5 pounds turbot bones (gills removed and iced)
For the Turbot Panises (Chick Pea Fritters):
Sea salt and pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
Preparation of the Braised Fennel:
1. Heat a heavy saucepan over high heat.
2. Add olive oil and fennel heads, sweating for one minute.
3. Season with salt and pepper, deglaze with chicken stock to cover.
4. Cover and bring to a boil. Simmer, covered, until fennel is tender (15 minutes).
5. Chill in stock over ice and set aside.
Preparation of the Confit Tomatoes:
1. Preheat oven to 275°F.
2. Boil tomatoes for ten seconds, then peel and quarter them.
3. Place in a pan with salt, oil, thyme, and garlic; cook at low temperature for three to four hours or until tomatoes are half their original size.
4. Reserve in the oil.
Preparation of the Preserved Lemon Zests:
1. Zest lemons without pith into 1/3-inch wide strips (2 inches long).
2. Boil zests in cold water, straining and repeating twice more.
3. Mix sugar, salt, lemon juice with syrup in a saucepan; add blanched zests.
4. Simmer for twenty minutes at low heat until zests are soft, tart, and sweet.
5. Refrigerate liquid up to one week.
Preparation of the Fritters:
1. Boil milk with butter; sift chick pea flour into mixture, whisking until very thick.
2. Spread on oiled sheet pan; place parchment on top, flatten with another sheet pan.
3. Chill and cut into 3x2-inch pieces.
4. Dredge in flour and fry in oil until crisp and golden brown.
Preparation of the Turbot Stock:
1. Heat a large-bottomed heavy saucepan over high flame.
2. Add olive oil; sweat fish bones (patted dry) for two minutes.
3. Add chopped shallots, fennel root, garlic, basil stems, and fennel sticks; sweat five minutes.
4. Cover with cold chicken stock; bring to a boil, skim, simmer gently for 45 minutes.
5. Strain through cheesecloth; cool.
Preparing the Sauce:
1. Reduce turbot stock to one-third gallon; add syrup from preserved lemons.
2. Season with sea salt and white pepper; reserve warm.
Presenting the Dish:
1. Season fillets with salt and white pepper, sear in hot pan with olive oil.
2. Warm fennel, olives, tomatoes, basil strips, and lemon zest under turbot fillets.
3. Finish with extra-virgin olive oil and serve.
